title: Fixing Unnecessary Re-renders with React Memo

statement.markdown: A React function component is re-rendering unnecessarily due to incorrect use of the `useMemo` hook. Fix the code to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an object with two properties: `name` and `age`.
statement.outputFormat: Output is an array of two strings: `name` and `age`.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the `age` property changes.

constraints.description: The number of times the component is rendered

examples.explanation: The component should only re-render when the age property changes.
The component should only re-render when the age property changes.

editorial.title: Fixing Unnecessary Re-renders with React Memo
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook is used to memoize a value, so it's only recalculated when one of its dependencies changes.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach involves recalculating the value on every render.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution involves using `useMemo` with a dependency array to memoize the value.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using `useMemo` with a dependency array to memoize the value, and only re-rendering when the dependency changes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the `useMemo` hook is used correctly to memoize the value.
editorial.dryRun: A step-by-step example run involves using the `useMemo` hook to memoize the value, and only re-rendering when the dependency changes.
commonMistakes: Using a dependency array with incorrect dependencies
